# Implementation Plan: 飞书 AI 机器人 (Feishu Agent Bot)

**Branch**: `001-feishu-agent-bot` | **Date**: 2026-04-30 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-feishu-agent-bot/spec.md`

**Note**: Artifacts produced by this plan command: [research.md](./research.md), [data-model.md](./data-model.md), [contracts/](./contracts/), [quickstart.md](./quickstart.md). `tasks.md` is generated by the next command (`/speckit.tasks`).

## Summary

一个部署在飞书工作空间的 Go 后端机器人：通过 @ (群聊) 或私聊接收消息，为每位用户独立维护 24 小时滑动会话上下文，驱动一个自研 agent loop（基于 cloudwego/eino 的 `ChatModel` 抽象层）完成"思考-文本-工具调用"三类流式产出，在单条飞书卡片内增量更新。工具包括网页搜索/抓取、飞书云文档读写（走 per-user OAuth `user_access_token`）、时间查询，以及通过 `mark3labs/mcp-go` 挂接的任意外部 MCP 工具。

**技术方法** (由 Phase 0 研究确定，见 [research.md](./research.md))：
- Go 1.26.2，eino 0.8.13 作为 LLM 多 provider 抽象层（**仅** `ChatModel`，不引入 ADK/compose/flow）。
- 飞书 SDK 使用 `larksuite/oapi-sdk-go/**v2**` v2.0.11（原方案文档中的 `/v3` 在上游无对应 tag，已更正）。
- 为支持 per-user OAuth (spec Q1 = B)，启动一个最小 HTTP 服务（`127.0.0.1:8080` 仅 `/oauth/start`、`/oauth/callback`、`/healthz`），由 TLS 反代对外发布；其余流量仍走 Feishu 长连接。
- 卡片流式更新走 `PATCH im/v1/messages/:id`，250 ms ticker 节流，响应 5 QPS/message 的 Feishu 官方限额；后续评估是否切换到 2024 新增的 Streaming Card API。
- 会话与锁存 Redis：`bot:sess:<key>` (24h 滑动 TTL)、`bot:lock:<key>` (`SET NX EX 60`)、`bot:oauth:<open_id>` (AES-GCM 加密，TTL 对齐 refresh token 过期)。
- Agent loop `MaxSteps = 12`，每工具 30 s 超时，工具错误以 `is_error=true` 的 tool 消息反馈回模型。

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Go 1.26.2 (latest stable per project policy)
**Primary Dependencies**:
  - `github.com/cloudwego/eino` v0.8.13 — LLM ChatModel abstraction
  - `github.com/cloudwego/eino-ext/components/model/{claude,openai,deepseek}` — per-subpackage tags (latest at `go get` time)
  - `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` v0.49.0 — MCP client (stdio / HTTP / SSE)
  - `github.com/larksuite/oapi-sdk-go/v3` v3.6.1 — Feishu long-connection + REST
  - `github.com/redis/go-redis/v9` v9.19.0 — session/lock/oauth store
  - `github.com/spf13/viper` v1.21.0 — YAML + env config
  - `github.com/stretchr/testify` v1.11.1 — tests
  - `codeberg.org/readeck/go-readability/v2` v2.1.1 — web-article extraction (maintained fork of archived go-shiori/go-readability)
**Storage**: Redis ≥ 7 (sessions, per-user locks, encrypted OAuth tokens); no RDBMS.
**Testing**: `testing` + `stretchr/testify`; `miniredis` for Redis unit tests; Feishu/LLM integration tests behind `-tags=integration`.
**Target Platform**: Linux amd64 (Docker container; single binary).
**Project Type**: Go backend service (single project layout).
**Performance Goals**:
  - P95 time-to-first-byte ≤ 3 s (spec SC-001).
  - P90 end-to-end for non-tool answers ≤ 10 s (spec SC-002).
  - Card update interval ≥ 250 ms (Feishu limit 5 QPS/message).
**Constraints**:
  - Card JSON ≤ 30 KB/message (Feishu hard cap).
  - Max 12 agent steps/turn (FR-043).
  - Per-tool execution ≤ 30 s.
  - Session context ≤ 40 messages per user (trimmed FIFO, system prompt regenerated).
  - OAuth tokens MUST be encrypted at rest (FR-047).
  - A single MCP server failure MUST NOT affect other tools (FR-062).
**Scale/Scope**: Designed for 1 Feishu tenant, up to ~200 DAU (team-size). Multi-instance horizontal scaling via Redis-backed shared state; single MCP subprocess set per instance. SLO: 99% monthly availability (SC-007).

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

`.specify/memory/constitution.md` is currently the **unfilled Spec-Kit placeholder template** (all principle sections still `[PRINCIPLE_N_NAME]`/`[PRINCIPLE_N_DESCRIPTION]`). A concrete constitution has not been ratified for this project.

**Gate evaluation**: No enforceable principles or gate rules are defined; therefore the constitution gate **passes trivially** — there are no conditions to violate. This fact is recorded here intentionally so a future constitution ratification (`/speckit.constitution`) can retroactively evaluate this plan against real principles.

**Pre-Phase 1 re-check**: same result — no principles to re-evaluate against.

**Recommendation (not blocking)**: run `/speckit.constitution` after M2 to codify at minimum: (a) "Never log secrets or raw tokens", (b) "Tool failures MUST be recovered-from, not propagated as loop errors", (c) "Persisted user data MUST be encrypted or non-sensitive". These are already load-bearing invariants in this plan; a constitution would make them first-class.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-feishu-agent-bot/
├── plan.md                    # This file
├── research.md                # Phase 0 output — decisions D1..D10 + open items
├── data-model.md              # Phase 1 output — entity catalog, invariants
├── quickstart.md              # Phase 1 output — dev + operator bring-up
├── contracts/                 # Phase 1 output — frozen contracts
│   ├── go-interfaces.md         (LLM, Tool, Session/Locker, MCP, Agent, Render, Lark, Config)
│   ├── http-endpoints.md        (OAuth callback server)
│   ├── tools.md                 (builtin tool JSON schemas)
│   └── feishu-card.md           (card JSON + state machine + rate limits)
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md        # Spec quality gate (all green after Q1=B applied)
└── tasks.md                   # Phase 2 output — NOT created by this command
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
cmd/
└── bot/
    └── main.go                # wires config → redis → llm → tools → mcp → lark → render → agent

internal/
├── agent/                     # Self-authored agent loop
│   ├── loop.go                # Run(ctx, sess, input, emit) — MaxSteps, tool dispatch, error feedback
│   ├── types.go               # internal Message/ToolCall aliases
│   └── context.go             # system-prompt composition, message trimming
├── llm/                       # Provider abstraction (on top of eino ChatModel)
│   ├── provider.go            # Provider interface + factory
│   ├── eino_adapter.go        # eino.ChatModel → llm.Provider (incl. ReasoningContent mapping)
│   └── types.go               # ChatRequest, StreamEvent, Usage
├── tool/
│   ├── tool.go                # Tool interface + Result
│   ├── registry.go            # name-unique registry, Available() filtering
│   └── builtin/
│       ├── web_search.go
│       ├── web_fetch.go
│       ├── feishu_doc_read.go
│       ├── feishu_doc_search.go
│       └── datetime.go
├── mcp/
│   ├── manager.go             # multi-server lifecycle, Status(), hot-reconnect
│   ├── client.go              # mcp-go wrapper
│   ├── stdio.go
│   ├── http.go
│   └── adapter.go             # mcp.Tool → tool.Tool, name-prefixing
├── session/
│   ├── store.go               # Store interface
│   ├── redis.go               # Redis implementation
│   ├── memory.go              # in-memory for unit tests
│   └── lock.go                # Locker (Redis SET NX + Lua release)
├── oauth/                     # NEW vs plan doc — required by Q1=B
│   ├── server.go              # HTTP handlers /oauth/start, /oauth/callback, /healthz
│   ├── state.go               # HMAC state token
│   ├── tokens.go              # AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt + refresh logic
│   └── store.go               # Redis-backed UserOAuthCredential CRUD
├── lark/
│   ├── handler.go             # event entry + command interception
│   ├── parser.go              # @ / command / mention parsing
│   ├── sender.go              # card send + PATCH
│   └── card.go                # card JSON templates (per feishu-card.md)
├── render/
│   ├── renderer.go            # Feed(events) → batched PATCH via 250ms ticker
│   └── state.go               # idle/thinking/text/tool_executing state machine
├── config/
│   └── config.go              # viper loader, env interpolation
└── log/
    └── log.go                 # slog wrapper; trace_id propagation

configs/
├── config.example.yaml
└── config.yaml                # gitignored

test/
├── integration/               # -tags=integration; hits real Redis + LLM
└── fixtures/                  # canned streams, Feishu event samples

Makefile
go.mod
go.sum
README.md
Dockerfile                     # M7
```

**Structure Decision**: **Single Go project, single binary** (`cmd/bot`). Flat `internal/` package tree with one package per bounded responsibility. The only departure from the original plan doc's tree is the addition of **`internal/oauth/`** — required because Q1 resolved to B (per-user OAuth). This package owns the public HTTP server and the encrypted-token lifecycle; it is the security-critical edge and is kept isolated from `internal/lark` and `internal/tool/builtin` behind a small interface (`oauth.Store.GetUserToken(open_id) (string, error)`).
